front: Opera: verismo opera set in the Latin Quarter of Paris among poor bohemian artists; Arturo Toscanini conducted its premiere in 1896
Opera: Jonathan Larson's musical Rent is based on this opera; the female lead in both is named Mimi; 'Musetta's Waltz' is referenced in the musical
Opera: based on Henri Murger's novel; librettist Giuseppe Giacosa resigned three times; Ruggero Leoncavallo accused Puccini of stealing the libretto and wrote his own competing version
Opera: characters skip out on the bill at the Café Momus using a military procession as cover; Colline complains no one accepts pledges on Christmas Eve
Opera: in Act I, Rodolfo burns his play for warmth and greets a musician who had been paid to play until an Englishman's parrot died
Opera character: seamstress who introduces herself in 'Mi chiamano Mimì'; dies of tuberculosis at the end; the female lead of Rent shares her name
Opera: Rodolfo hides Mimi's key when her candle goes out, then sings 'Che gelida manina' (Your Tiny Hand is Frozen) to keep her close
Opera: Mimi receives a pink bonnet and laments her lover's abandonment in 'Donde lieta uscì'
Opera duet: Rodolfo sees Mimi's face bathed in moonlight; ends with the tenor singing a unison perdendosi high C; opened by three descending chords
work: "O soave fanciulla" and the Act I Love Duet

front: Opera aria: sung in 3/4 time by the flirtatious companion of Marcello at the Café Momus; she boasts 'When I walk alone in the street, people stop and stare at me'; intended to make her lover jealous
back: "Quando me'n vo" (Musetta's Waltz) from La Bohème (Puccini)

front: Opera aria: Musetta pretends her shoe hurts to send away her rich admirer Alcindoro; then the group leaves the café without paying
back: "Quando me'n vo" (Musetta's Waltz) from La Bohème (Puccini)

front: Opera aria: Colline sings this while pawning his overcoat to buy medicine for the dying Mimi
